question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile where blockFile.hasRecordFile() returns true, causing transform() to return the embedded RecordFile directly without any fee validation, allowing an arbitrarily inflated or zeroed transactionFeeCharged to be persisted to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile whose embedded RecordFile contains a transactionFee value exceeding the transactionBody.getTransactionFee() max-fee cap, bypassing the hasRecordFile() fast-path with no upper-bound check, causing the mirror node to record a fee higher than the user-authorized maximum?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a BlockFile containing BlockTransaction items whose transactionResult.getTransactionFeeCharged() values are set to zero or negative, causing getRecordItems() to build RecordItems with a zero or negative chargedTxFee that is then persisted, misrepresenting the actual fee charged?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getTransactionFeeCharged() exceeds Long.MAX_VALUE / 2, causing integer overflow when the fee is accumulated or compared downstream, leading to incorrect fee records in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a BlockTransaction with a transactionResult.getCongestionPricingMultiplier() of zero or a negative value, causing the congestionPricingMultiplier field on the resulting RecordItem to be set outside its valid design range, corrupting fee multiplier records without any bounds check in getRecordItems()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getHighVolumePricingMultiplier() is set to an arbitrarily large value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE), causing the highVolumePricingMultiplier to be stored in the mirror node database without any upper-bound validation, misrepresenting the actual pricing multiplier applied?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile where a child BlockTransaction's transactionResult.getTransactionFeeCharged() is set to a value greater than its parent transaction's fee, exploiting the absence of parent-child fee consistency validation in getRecordItems(), causing the mirror node to record inconsistent fee data for child transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with BlockTransactions processed in reverse order where the transactionIndex field is set to the original list index rather than the reversed processing index, causing fee attribution to be associated with the wrong transaction index in the persisted record, leading to incorrect fee reporting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with an empty BlockTransaction list that bypasses getRecordItems() processing entirely via the isEmpty() check, then supply a hasRecordFile() path with manipulated fees, causing the transform() method to return a RecordFile with arbitrary fee values without any transaction-level fee validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a BlockTransaction with a transactionResult.getAssessedCustomFeesList() containing entries with negative amounts, causing addAllAssessedCustomFees() to persist negative custom fee records that misrepresent the actual fees assessed, effectively showing fee refunds that never occurred?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getAssessedCustomFeesList() contains entries with amounts exceeding the token's configured fee schedule, exploiting the absence of fee schedule validation in getRecordItems(), causing the mirror node to record custom fees that exceed design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getTransferList() contains transfers that do not sum to zero, exploiting the absence of transfer-list balance validation in getRecordItems(), causing the mirror node to persist an unbalanced transfer list that misrepresents fee distribution?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getTransferList() omits the node fee transfer entry, causing the mirror node to record a transfer list that does not reflect the actual node fee charged, misrepresenting fee distribution to node operators?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getTransferList() omits the network/service fee transfer to the treasury account (0.0.98), causing the mirror node to record a transfer list that does not reflect the actual service fee charged?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getPaidStakingRewardsList() contains entries with inflated reward amounts, exploiting the absence of staking reward validation in getRecordItems(), causing the mirror node to record staking rewards that exceed design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getTransactionFeeCharged() is set to a value greater than transactionBody.getTransactionFee() (the user-authorized max fee), exploiting the absence of a max-fee cap check in getRecordItems(), causing the mirror node to record a fee that exceeds what the user authorized?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with multiple BlockTransactions sharing the same consensusTimestamp, causing the reverse-order processing in getRecordItems() to incorrectly resolve parent-child relationships via parseParent(), leading to fee attribution being assigned to the wrong parent transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getConsensusTimestamp() does not match the expected block ordering, causing the reverse-order processing in getRecordItems() to build RecordItems with incorrect previous links, leading to fee records being associated with wrong transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a null or empty blockHeader.getHapiProtoVersion(), causing transform() to construct a RecordFile with a zero hapiVersion (0.0.0), which may disable version-gated fee validation logic downstream, allowing fees outside design parameters to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a blockHeader.getSoftwareVersion() set to a very old version number, causing transform() to build a RecordFile with a downgraded softwareVersion, which may trigger legacy fee processing paths downstream that apply less strict fee validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getCongestionPricingMultiplier() is set to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the congestionPricingMultiplier to overflow when used in fee calculations downstream, resulting in incorrect fee records being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getHighVolumePricingMultiplier() is set to zero, exploiting the absence of a minimum-value check, causing the mirror node to record a zero high-volume pricing multiplier that misrepresents the actual pricing applied to the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a failed BlockTransaction (status != SUCCESS) whose transactionResult.getTransactionFeeCharged() is set to a non-zero value, exploiting the absence of a check that failed transactions should have zero or minimal fees, causing the mirror node to record an inflated fee for a failed transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getAssessedCustomFeesList() contains entries referencing non-existent token IDs, exploiting the absence of token ID validation in getRecordItems(), causing the mirror node to persist custom fee records for tokens that do not exist?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with a BlockTransaction whose transactionResult.getAssessedCustomFeesList() contains entries referencing non-existent collector account IDs, exploiting the absence of collector account validation in getRecordItems(), causing the mirror node to persist custom fee records with invalid collector accounts?
